title: Local opinions about air quality
summary: This column was written by Nathan Kettle, an International Arctic Research Center geographer, and first published in the Fairbanks Daily News-Miner . It was part of a series of articles on air quality research by the UAF-led Fairbanks winter air study, commonly called “Fair Air.” Fairbanks, North Pole and surrounding areas frequently experience unhealthy levels of particulate matter in winter air.
